articles.title: Gemini can now pull from Google Photos to generate personalized images
articles.summary: Google's Gemini now generates personalized images using data from Google Photos through its Personal Intelligence feature. Users can create contextual images like dream houses that reflect their individual tastes and lifestyle.

articles.title: Anthropic releases a new Opus model amid Mythos Preview buzz
articles.summary: Anthropic released Claude Opus 4.7, its most powerful generally available model, featuring improvements in software engineering, image analysis, and creative document generation. The release follows the recent announcement of Mythos Preview, a cybersecurity-focused model.

articles.title: Ronan Farrow on Sam Altman’s ‘unconstrained’ relationship with the truth
articles.summary: Investigative journalist Ronan Farrow discusses his New Yorker investigation into OpenAI CEO Sam Altman's trustworthiness and relationship with truth. The feature examines Altman's credibility amid OpenAI's rise.

articles.title: InsightFinder raises $15M to help companies figure out where AI agents go wrong
articles.summary: InsightFinder raised $15M to help companies diagnose AI agent failures across their entire tech stack, addressing the industry's challenge of monitoring AI systems and their integration.

articles.title: AI traffic to US retailers rose 393% in Q1, and it’s boosting their revenue too
articles.summary: AI traffic to US retail sites surged 393% in Q1, with March alone seeing 269% growth. AI shoppers convert better and generate more revenue than non-AI visitors.

articles.title: Roblox’s AI assistant gets new agentic tools to plan, build, and test games
articles.summary: Roblox introduces new agentic AI tools enabling creators to plan, build, and test games throughout the entire development process.

articles.title: Railway secures $100 million to challenge AWS with AI-native cloud infrastructure
articles.summary: Railway, a cloud platform with 2 million developers, raised $100 million to build AI-native infrastructure challenging AWS and Google Cloud by addressing legacy platforms' limitations.

articles.title: Claude Code costs up to $200 a month. Goose does the same thing for free.
articles.summary: Claude Code charges up to $200/month for AI coding assistance, but free open-source alternative Goose offers similar functionality without subscription fees or cloud dependency.

articles.title: Listen Labs raises $69M after viral billboard hiring stunt to scale AI customer interviews
articles.summary: Listen Labs raised $69M Series B after a viral billboard hiring campaign featuring AI tokens and a coding challenge. The creative recruitment stunt helped the startup attract top engineering talent in a competitive market.

articles.title: “Negative” views of Broadcom driving thousands of VMware migrations, rival says
articles.summary: Nutanix claims to have attracted thousands of VMware customers due to dissatisfaction with Broadcom's VMware takeover. The rival company now has about 30,000 customers, many migrating from VMware.

articles.title: Iran-linked hackers disrupt operations at US critical infrastructure sites
articles.summary: Iranian government-linked hackers are disrupting US critical infrastructure by targeting programmable logic controllers in factories, water treatment centers, and oil refineries. Multiple US agencies warn of operational disruptions and financial losses since March 2026.

articles.title: Thousands of consumer routers hacked by Russia's military
articles.summary: Russia's military intelligence agency GRU hacked 18,000-40,000 consumer routers globally to redirect users to credential-harvesting sites for espionage operations.

articles.title: Making AI operational in constrained public sector environments
articles.summary: Public sector organizations face pressure to adopt AI but must navigate unique security and governance constraints. Small language models offer a practical solution for implementing AI in government environments while addressing data protection concerns.

articles.title: Treating enterprise AI as an operating layer
articles.summary: Enterprise AI's competitive advantage lies in building an operating layer—combining software, data, feedback loops, and governance—rather than just accessing foundation models as utilities.

articles.title: The Download: cyberscammers’ banking bypasses, and carbon removal troubles
articles.summary: Cyberscammers are using illicit hacking tools sold on Telegram to bypass banks' KYC facial recognition security, enabling fraudulent account access and money laundering operations.

articles.title: The PR you would have opened yourself
articles.summary: Article discusses how AI systems can autonomously generate pull requests developers would have created themselves, improving development efficiency through predictive code contributions.

articles.title: Training and Finetuning Multimodal Embedding & Reranker Models with Sentence Transformers
articles.summary: Sentence Transformers now supports training multimodal embedding and reranker models, enabling users to create custom models combining text and images for improved search and ranking tasks.

articles.title: Inside VAKRA: Reasoning, Tool Use, and Failure Modes of Agents
articles.summary: VAKRA explores how AI agents reason, utilize tools, and encounter failures. The research examines agent capabilities and limitations in complex task execution.

articles.title: A conversation with Kevin Scott: What’s next in AI
articles.summary: Kevin Scott discusses the future direction of AI technology and its potential applications. The conversation explores upcoming developments in artificial intelligence and emerging trends.

articles.title: From Hot Wheels to handling content: How brands are using Microsoft AI to be more productive and imaginative
articles.summary: Brands like Hot Wheels leverage Microsoft AI tools to enhance productivity and boost creativity in content creation and business operations.

articles.title: Microsoft open sources its ‘farm of the future’ toolkit
articles.summary: Microsoft has open sourced its 'farm of the future' toolkit, making agricultural technology tools publicly available for developers and farmers to improve farming practices through AI and technology.

articles.title: New ways to create personalized images in the Gemini app
articles.summary: Nano Banana 2 now generates personalized images using your Google Photos and personal context, creating visuals that reflect your unique life and experiences.

articles.title: Gemini 3.1 Flash TTS: the next generation of expressive AI speech
articles.summary: Google launches Gemini 3.1 Flash TTS, an advanced text-to-speech model offering expressive AI-generated voice capabilities. The technology is now available across Google's product ecosystem.

articles.title: Turn your best AI prompts into one-click tools in Chrome
articles.summary: Chrome Skills feature enables users to discover, save, and remix AI workflows for instant reuse, converting frequently used prompts into convenient one-click tools.

articles.title: Gemini Robotics-ER 1.6: Powering real-world robotics tasks through enhanced embodied reasoning
articles.summary: Gemini Robotics ER 1.6 enhances spatial reasoning and multi-view understanding for autonomous robots, enabling them to perform real-world tasks more effectively through improved embodied reasoning capabilities.

articles.title: Gemma 4: Byte for byte, the most capable open models
articles.summary: Google released Gemma 4, its most capable open-source models designed for advanced reasoning and agentic workflows, offering improved intelligence and performance.

articles.title: LWiAI Podcast #238 - GPT 5.4 mini, OpenAI Pivot, Mamba 3, Attention Residuals
articles.summary: OpenAI released GPT-5.4 mini and nano models with 400k-token context windows and higher per-token prices but improved token efficiency. Mistral open-sourced the Small 4 model family MoE with 119B parameters.

articles.title: Last Week in AI #339 - DLSS 5, OpenAI Superapp, MiniMax M2.7
articles.summary: Nvidia unveiled DLSS 5, an AI-powered graphics technology that enhances real-time photorealism in video games up to 4K by analyzing scene semantics and lighting to generate detailed visuals.

articles.title: LWiAI Podcast #237 - Nemotron 3 Super, xAI reborn, Anthropic Lawsuit, Research!
articles.summary: Episode 237 discusses major AI developments including Perplexity's local Mac-based AI agent, Anthropic's GitHub PR code review pricing, and ChatGPT's new interactive math/science visuals alongside other recent industry advances.

articles.title: 5 top cloud migration software for Infrastructure as Code (IaC)
articles.summary: Cloud migration requires Infrastructure as Code to ensure reproducible, deployment-ready environments. Platforms like Infros help teams design and validate optimized cloud architectures before deployment.

articles.title: OpenAI Agents SDK improves governance with sandbox execution
articles.summary: OpenAI's Agents SDK now features sandbox execution, enabling enterprises to deploy automated workflows with controlled risk and improved governance while maintaining flexibility and security.

articles.title: Cadence expands AI and robotic partnerships with Nvidia, Google Cloud
articles.summary: Cadence Design Systems expanded AI partnerships with Nvidia and Google Cloud, integrating physics-based simulation with accelerated computing for robotic systems and semiconductor design through Nvidia's CUDA-X and Omniverse platforms.

articles.title: Which Agent Causes Task Failures and When?Researchers from PSU and Duke explores automated failure attribution of LLM Multi-Agent Systems
articles.summary: Researchers from PSU and Duke developed methods to automatically identify which agent causes task failures in LLM multi-agent systems and when failures occur, addressing a key challenge in collaborative AI systems.

articles.title: ByteDance Introduces Astra: A Dual-Model Architecture for Autonomous Robot Navigation
articles.summary: ByteDance introduces Astra, a dual-model architecture for autonomous robot navigation that addresses key challenges like localization and path planning in complex indoor environments, enabling more capable mobile robots.

articles.title: MIT Researchers Unveil “SEAL”: A New Step Towards Self-Improving AI
articles.summary: MIT researchers introduced SEAL, a framework enabling large language models to self-improve by generating training data through self-editing and updating their own weights using reinforcement learning.

articles.title: The paradox of LLM self-distillation: Faster reasoning, weaker generalization
articles.summary: Self-distillation optimizes LLMs for speed but reduces their ability to explore solutions on complex problems, revealing a tradeoff between efficiency and generalization.

articles.title: Why harness engineering is becoming the new AI moat
articles.summary: As large language models become commoditized, the engineering infrastructure surrounding them emerges as the real competitive advantage, as evidenced by Anthropic's Claude Code leak.

articles.title: How GhostClaw malware targets the OpenClaw AI agent boom
articles.summary: GhostClaw malware exploits macOS binaries to steal credentials from developers running local AI agents on Mac Minis, targeting the growing OpenClaw AI agent ecosystem.

articles.title: UCSD and Together AI Research Introduces Parcae: A Stable Architecture for Looped Language Models That Achieves the Quality of a Transformer Twice the Size
articles.summary: UCSD and Together AI introduce Parcae, a stable looped transformer architecture that achieves transformer-quality performance at half the model size, addressing the challenge of scaling model quality without increasing memory footprint.

articles.title: How to Build a Universal Long-Term Memory Layer for AI Agents Using Mem0 and OpenAI
articles.summary: This tutorial demonstrates building persistent long-term memory for AI agents using Mem0, OpenAI, and ChromaDB. It enables semantic storage, retrieval, and personalized responses with CRUD control and multi-user isolation.

articles.title: A Coding Implementation to Build Multi-Agent AI Systems with SmolAgents Using Code Execution, Tool Calling, and Dynamic Orchestration
articles.summary: Tutorial on building production-ready multi-agent AI systems using SmolAgents, demonstrating code execution, tool calling, and dynamic orchestration across lightweight agents.

articles.title: Want to build a startup that gets acquired? This founder shares 5 proven tips
articles.summary: Fintech founder Jem Walters shares five proven strategies that helped him build and successfully sell his app Snoop, offering insights for entrepreneurs targeting acquisition.

articles.title: Google to pay $135M settlement to Android phone users - how to claim your share if you qualify
articles.summary: Google settles a $135M lawsuit over unauthorized data transmission on Android phones. Users can file claims to receive compensation if they qualify.

articles.title: MacBook Neo vs. Surface: Why spiraling RAM prices are bruising Microsoft's PC business but not Apple's
articles.summary: Rising RAM and storage prices hurt Microsoft's Surface line significantly, but Apple's MacBook remains unaffected. Apple's vertical integration and pricing strategy insulate it from market pressures affecting competitors.

articles.title: Python Decorators for Production Machine Learning Engineering
articles.summary: Article discusses using Python decorators in production machine learning engineering to improve code quality and functionality.

articles.title: 5 Techniques for Efficient Long-Context RAG
articles.summary: Article discusses five techniques for improving efficiency in long-context Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) systems, enabling better handling of extended document contexts with optimized performance.

articles.title: How to Implement Tool Calling with Gemma 4 and Python
articles.summary: This article explains how to implement tool calling functionality using Gemma 4 and Python, demonstrating techniques for leveraging open-weights models in practical applications.

articles.title: Your Chunks Failed Your RAG in Production
articles.summary: Chunking decisions in RAG systems are critical upstream choices that no LLM can fix once implemented incorrectly in production. Poor chunking strategies fundamentally limit retrieval-augmented generation performance.

articles.title: Building My Own Personal AI Assistant: A Chronicle, Part 2
articles.summary: The author adds a task breaker module to their personal AI assistant that decomposes complex goals into actionable steps, significantly improving AI-driven productivity workflows.

articles.title: memweave: Zero-Infra AI Agent Memory with Markdown and SQLite — No Vector Database Required
articles.summary: Memweave offers a lightweight AI agent memory solution using Markdown and SQLite, eliminating the need for vector databases or complex infrastructure.

articles.title: No Need for Space Gear — Capcom’s ‘PRAGMATA’ Joins GeForce NOW on Launch Day
articles.summary: Capcom's sci-fi action game PRAGMATA launches on GeForce NOW the same day as its worldwide release, allowing players to stream it on any device without dedicated hardware.

articles.title: Rethinking AI TCO: Why Cost per Token Is the Only Metric That Matters
articles.summary: Data centers have transformed into AI token factories prioritizing inference workloads. Enterprises should shift focus from traditional metrics like FLOPS per dollar to cost per token for accurate TCO assessment.

articles.title: New Adobe Premiere Color Grading Mode Accelerated on NVIDIA GPUs
articles.summary: Adobe announces a new Premiere Color Mode in beta at NAB Show 2026, accelerated by NVIDIA RTX technology to enhance video editing workflows for professional creators.

articles.title: Create rich, custom tooltips in Amazon Quick Sight
articles.summary: Amazon QuickSight now enables custom tooltips using free-form layout sheets, allowing dashboard authors to design interactive tooltips combining charts, KPIs, and visuals that dynamically render on data point hover.

articles.title: Accelerating decode-heavy LLM inference with speculative decoding on AWS Trainium and vLLM
articles.summary: AWS Trainium with speculative decoding accelerates LLM token generation by up to 3x for decode-heavy workloads. A draft model proposes multiple tokens while the target model verifies them, reducing inference costs and improving throughput.

articles.title: Rede Mater Dei de Saúde: Monitoring AI agents in the revenue cycle with Amazon Bedrock AgentCore
articles.summary: Rede Mater Dei de Saúde implements 12 AI agents using Amazon Bedrock AgentCore to monitor and govern multi-agent systems in healthcare revenue cycles, improving operational sustainability and decision-making.

articles.title: How to Build Vision AI Pipelines Using DeepStream Coding Agents
articles.summary: DeepStream Coding Agents simplify building real-time vision AI pipelines by reducing complex data pipeline development and extensive coding requirements for developers.

articles.title: Building Custom Atomistic Simulation Workflows for Chemistry and Materials Science with NVIDIA ALCHEMI Toolkit
articles.summary: NVIDIA's ALCHEMI Toolkit enables custom atomistic simulation workflows for chemistry and materials science, balancing computational accuracy and speed. It addresses the longstanding challenge between expensive ab initio methods and faster but less accurate approaches.

articles.title: NVIDIA NVbandwidth: Your Essential Tool for Measuring GPU Interconnect and Memory Performance
articles.summary: NVIDIA NVbandwidth is a tool that measures GPU memory and interconnect performance for CUDA applications, helping developers optimize data transfer in single and multi-GPU systems.

articles.title: How generational differences affect consumer attitudes towards ads
articles.summary: A research study examines how different generations perceive and respond to social media advertisements across various platforms, revealing generational differences in consumer attitudes toward ads.

articles.title: Every tree counts
articles.summary: Meta aims for net zero emissions by 2030, developing carbon mitigation technology and sharing it publicly.

articles.title: How a non-traditional background led to cutting-edge XR tech
articles.summary: A professional with unconventional background pioneered innovative XR technology developments, demonstrating how diverse experience can drive breakthrough advancements in emerging tech fields.

articles.title: Identifying Interactions at Scale for LLMs
articles.summary: Researchers propose methods to identify feature interactions in Large Language Models at scale, advancing interpretability and understanding of LLM decision-making processes for safer AI systems.

articles.title: Information-Driven Design of Imaging Systems
articles.summary: Research presents information-driven approach to designing imaging systems that optimize how well measurements distinguish objects. Uses noisy measurements and noise models to improve imaging system performance.

articles.title: RL without TD learning
articles.summary: A new RL algorithm uses divide-and-conquer instead of temporal difference learning, avoiding TD scalability issues and performing better on long-horizon tasks.
